title: Repeating coil
content: In telecommunication s, a repeating coil is a voice-frequency transformer characterized by a closed magnetic core, a pair of identical balanced primary (line) winding s, a pair of identical but not necessarily balanced secondary (drop) windings, and low transmission loss at voice frequencies. It permits transfer of voice current s from one winding to another by magnetic induction, matches line and drop impedance s, and prevents direct conduction between the line and the drop. It is a special application of an isolation transformer, and is often used to prevent ground loop s or earth loops, which cause humming or buzzing in audio circuits. It also prevents low direct current voltage s from passing.
